sourceMovie.title: Tetris
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Tetris (2023) as a thriller theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The thriller and history blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Courier
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Tetris clicked for you, The Courier (2020) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and history, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Need for Speed
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Tetris clicked for you, Need for Speed (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Tetris?
faq.answer: Try The Courier, Need for Speed, WarGames—each shares DNA with Tetris while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Tetris?
faq.answer: Tetris (2023) is best described as Thriller, History, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Tetris worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
